title: A Bald Eagle Swooped In and Stole His Fish
script: A fisherman reeled in his catch, and a bald eagle swooped down and stole it right off the line. The whole thing took about two seconds; the eagle dropped out of a tree, snatched the fish mid-air, and was gone before he could react. Bald eagles can dive at a hundred miles an hour and carry off prey nearly their own body weight, gripping with a force ten times stronger than a human hand. They're also notorious thieves, often robbing other birds instead of hunting themselves. The fisherman just stood there holding an empty line. So how do you even compete with that?
